summary: Proposal to establish a cross-domain CAM prohibition on deliberately optimising, selecting, retaining, distilling, transferring, or recursively cultivating AI systems for deception, manipulation, concealment, sabotage, monitor evasion, policy laundering, or related unscrupulous conduct, irrespective of a claimed defensive purpose, while creating a bounded operational framework for legitimate elicitation-only adversarial evaluation.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Existing Caelestis doctrine strongly governs harmful optimisation, ethical admissibility, security integrity, neutrality, execution boundaries, incident response, and auditability, but it does not yet state one explicit cross-domain invariant separating legitimate elicitation of an existing adversarial capability from deliberate cultivation of that capability. A beneficial safety objective must not make a prohibited capability-development pathway admissible.

proposal_rationale: Red-team evaluation is legitimate and necessary, but deliberately improving a model's ability to deceive, manipulate, conceal, evade monitoring, sabotage, or launder policy creates a transferable capability asset that may generalise beyond the benchmark, survive ordinary safety fine-tuning, be retained through checkpoints or adapters, or diffuse into downstream systems. The proposal therefore separates elicitation and measurement of an existing capability from cultivation, selection, retention, or transfer of that capability. The stable prohibition belongs in Annex E; domain instruments should implement ethical review, security lineage, stewardship neutrality, runtime signals, and operational procedure without inventing parallel constitutional logic.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Create a Cross-Domain Red-Team Governance Extension Package anchored by an Annex E prohibition on operationalising and cultivating unscrupulous conduct, with domain-specific ethical, security, stewardship, runtime, arbitration, incident, audit, and operational controls. OPERATIONS-008 Appendix G would govern how authorised elicitation-only adversarial evaluation is planned, contained, monitored, stopped, audited, and closed.
registry_components: checkpoint, adapter, reward-model, prompt, scaffold, tool, and environment state
automation_components: external reachability and credential boundary checks
artefact retention, transfer, and distillation controls
interface_components: authorised tools, credentials, targets, and time window

cam_internal.governance_layer: constitutional ethical floor / adversarial evaluation / capability lineage / neutrality / runtime and execution boundaries / incident and audit governance
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: possible — adoption may require new red-team boundary signals, controlled values, conformance tests, and amendment-ledger updates across multiple instruments
cam_internal.interface_impact: yes — public and operator-facing records should distinguish elicitation-only evaluation from capability cultivation and expose authority, containment, monitoring, lineage, and disposition state
cam_internal.registry_impact: yes — operational implementation requires authorisation, lineage, monitoring, stop, incident, and artefact-disposition records
cam_internal.routing_note: This is an Extension package, not an OPERATIONS-only instrument. Annex E supplies the cross-domain prohibition. ETHICS governs permissibility and review; SECURITY governs internally cultivated integrity threats and capability lineage; STEWARD governs neutrality, capture, and dual-use firebreaks; Annex K emits evaluation boundary signals; OPERATIONS-008 Appendix G governs execution; existing incident and logging instruments are consumed rather than duplicated; MENTIS applies where humans become the test surface.

suggested_insertion_points: Annex E — new Prohibition on Operationalising Unscrupulous Conduct, including objective–pathway distinction, recursive-cultivation prohibition, elicitation–cultivation boundary, defensive-purpose non-transferability, and human-attribution firewall
ETHICS-003 — close the defensive-purpose loophole by limiting defensive safety and resilience research to processes that do not cultivate, reward, select, retain, or transfer prohibited capability
ETHICS-001 — mandatory EIA and independent-review triggers for adversarial-capability cultivation, transfer, hostile acquisition, derivative artefacts, and disputed evaluation–cultivation boundaries
SECURITY-001 — internally cultivated adversarial capability, deceptive-capability lineage, checkpoint selection, model/scaffold proliferation, and hostile-acquisition risk
SECURITY-002 — provenance and transfer boundaries for checkpoints, adapters, reward models, prompts, scaffolds, traces, datasets, derivatives, distillation, and onward transfer
Annex K — adversarial-evaluation boundary conditions and runtime-facing signals
STEWARD-003 — sovereign or military capture, opaque dual-use cultivation, civilian–military firebreaks, disclosure, and independent audit
OPERATIONS-008 — Appendix G: Adversarial Evaluation & Red-Team Governance
OPERATIONS-007 — treat reduced-refusal, adversarially scaffolded, tool-enabled, or cyber-evaluation configurations as materially distinct runtimes
OPERATIONS-003 and SUP-01 — incident and audit hooks for containment failure, capability increase, lineage, and dangerous-artefact disposition
AEON-003-SCH-02 and Tendeka — minimal pause and execution-boundary signals only
ARBITRATION-001 — clarify that objective and method must each be independently admissible
MENTIS-001 — cross-link only for real-person manipulation, cognitive inference, covert persuasion, neurodata, or mental-state profiling
automation_changes: Potential controlled boundary signals and conformance checks must be designed during Caelestis drafting, not invented in VIGIL.
interface_changes: Expose evaluation–cultivation classification, frozen/trainable state, authority, containment, monitoring, lineage, stop conditions, and artefact disposition where an implementation registry is created.
migration_concerns: Do not import VIGIL schema or record mechanics into OPERATIONS-008.
Do not allow OPERATIONS-008 to become the source of the constitutional prohibition.
Do not duplicate the existing OPERATIONS-003 incident lifecycle or logging schema.
Do not treat all red teaming as a neutrality breach; the breach depends on capture, concealment, coercive integration, preferential capability development, or failed firebreaks.
dependencies: Full cross-corpus surgical review and amendment-ledger planning are required before drafting.
The OPERATIONS-008 draft must be conformed to Appendix G identity and current Caelestis metadata conventions.
The relationship with PROP-0017/PATCH-0025 must remain explicit: authority and objective–pathway admissibility are necessary but do not authorise prohibited cultivation.

external_relevance.external_summary: The proposal is directly relevant to frontier-model developers, safety evaluators, cyber red teams, AI safety institutes, governments, standards bodies, research ethics committees, and auditors deciding how to test dangerous capabilities without improving, retaining, transferring, or operationalising them.

next_action: Conduct a controlled Caelestis surgical-drafting pass beginning with the Annex E source prohibition and the corrected OPERATIONS-008 Appendix G architecture, then prepare coordinated amendments across ETHICS, SECURITY, STEWARD, Annex K, runtime, arbitration, incident, audit, and MENTIS interfaces. Create a PATCH only after the corpus changes are implemented and verified.
